parameters.content:
## Prompt Chaining

### How it works

This workflow chains multiple AI prompts around an email-driven document processing flow. It starts from a Gmail trigger, analyzes and extracts structured information from the document, validates the result, posts it to an external webhook, and then uses another AI Agent to prepare and send a Gmail follow-up.

### Setup steps

- Connect Gmail credentials for the Gmail Trigger and Gmail sending tool, and configure the trigger criteria for the incoming documents to process.
- Configure Google Gemini credentials for the Gemini analysis node and both AI Agent model subnodes, including any fallback model settings.
- Review the Structured Output Parser schema so it matches the fields expected by the validation code and webhook payload.
- Update the HTTP Request node with the correct webhook or API endpoint, method, headers, and authentication if needed.
- Review the Validate Document Data code node to ensure its validation rules match the document types and required fields.

### Customization

You can adjust the extraction prompt and parser schema for different document formats, change the validation logic for stricter business rules, replace the webhook URL with a production API, and customize the second AI Agent’s Gmail message tone or content.

## Ingest and extract document

Starts when a Gmail event occurs, analyzes the incoming document with Gemini, and uses an AI Agent with primary/fallback Gemini models plus a structured output parser to extract normalized document data. This forms the left-side AI extraction cluster.

## Validate and submit data

Runs custom validation on the extracted document fields and then sends the validated payload to an external webhook endpoint via HTTP POST. These nodes sit in the middle of the canvas as the handoff between extraction and follow-up processing.

## Generate Gmail follow-up

Uses a second AI Agent with primary/fallback Gemini chat models and a Gmail tool to generate and send a follow-up Gmail message after the external submission step. This is the right-side response/action cluster.

parameters.text:
Analyze the document and structure the content to markdown:

## SID
Company Name
## SID City/State/Zip
SID company City State and zip code
## SID Address
SID Company address
## SID Name
SID Company Name


## CID
Company Name
## CID City/State/Zip
CID Customer City State and zip code
## CID Address
CID Customer address
## CID Name
CID Customer Name


## Bill of Lading Number
The BOL number

## Carrier Name
Name of the freight carrier company
## Trailer number
number of the trailer
## Seal number
ID of the Seal

## Customer Order Information Table
| CUSTOMER ORDER NUMBER      | # PKGS             | WEIGHT        | PALLET/SLIP (CIRCLE ONE) | ADDITIONAL SHIPPER INFO    |
|----------------------------|--------------------|---------------|--------------------------|----------------------------|
| order id starting with ORD | number of packages | weight in lbs | Y/N                      | description of the article |

## Carrier Information Table
| HANDLING UNIT QTY         | HANDLING UNIT TYPE        | PACKAGE QTY      | PACKAGE TYPE | WEIGHT WEIGHT      | H.M.■(X) | COMMODITY DESCRIPTION COMMODITY DESCRIPTION | LTL ONLY NMFC # | LTL ONLY CLASS |
|---------------------------|---------------------------|------------------|--------------|--------------------|----------|---------------------------------------------|-----------------|----------------|
| Quantity of handling unit | Type of the handling unit | Package qunatity | Package type | Weight of the unit | H.M      | Description of the Unit                     | NMFC number     | LTL class      |


## Except as noted Date
Date of the Carrier exeptance in the signature area

options.systemMessage: Structure the user message and return valid JSON using the given JSON schema. Make sure to only output the JSON and nothing else.

You are an agent that answers emails. You will receive data from a workflow, that checked if an bill of lading has all necessary information in it. You only get messages where all is fine. Be sure to write a nice message of acceptance and list the items. Be sure to structure the message with a nice HTML layout. You will use your GMAIL tool for sending the message, which accpets HTML.

parameters.pythonCode:
# Loop over input items and add a new field called 'my_new_field' to the JSON of each one
for item in _items:
    customer_orders = item["json"].get("customer_orders", [])
    carrier_line_items = item["json"].get("carrier_line_items", [])

    mismatches = []

    for order in customer_orders:
        order_number = order.get("customer_order_number")
        order_pkgs = order.get("packages")
        order_weight = order.get("weight_lbs")

        # Find matching carrier line item by commodity description or index
        matched_carrier = next(
            (c for c in carrier_line_items if c.get("package", {}).get("qty") == order_pkgs),
            None
        )

        if matched_carrier is None:
            mismatches.append({
                "order_number": order_number,
                "reason": "No matching carrier line item found",
                "order_packages": order_pkgs,
                "order_weight_lbs": order_weight
            })
        else:
            carrier_pkgs = matched_carrier.get("package", {}).get("qty")
            carrier_weight = matched_carrier.get("weight_lbs")

            pkg_match = order_pkgs == carrier_pkgs
            weight_match = order_weight == carrier_weight

            if not pkg_match or not weight_match:
                mismatches.append({
                    "order_number": order_number,
                    "reason": "Package count or weight mismatch",
                    "order_packages": order_pkgs,
                    "carrier_packages": carrier_pkgs,
                    "packages_match": pkg_match,
                    "order_weight_lbs": order_weight,
                    "carrier_weight_lbs": carrier_weight,
                    "weight_match": weight_match
                })

    all_match = len(mismatches) == 0

    item["json"]["validation"] = {
        "all_match": all_match,
        "mismatches": mismatches if not all_match else []
    }

return _items
